seoTitle: How to Record Depreciation in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record depreciation in QuickBooks Online so your fixed assets reflect their true current value on your financial statements.
intro: As business assets age, their book value drops, and recording that depreciation keeps your financial statements accurate. This guide covers how to record depreciation for an asset in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Confirm your fixed asset accounts
steps.body: Make sure the asset you're depreciating has a corresponding fixed asset account set up.

steps.heading: Set up a depreciation expense account
steps.body: Create or confirm a dedicated account for tracking depreciation expense.

steps.heading: Create a journal entry
steps.body: Record a journal entry that debits depreciation expense and credits accumulated depreciation for the asset.

steps.heading: Repeat on your depreciation schedule
steps.body: Enter the journal entry on a recurring basis, such as monthly or annually, according to your depreciation method.

faq.question: Does QuickBooks Online calculate depreciation automatically?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online doesn't automatically calculate depreciation for you, so the amount is usually determined outside the software and entered manually.

faq.question: What depreciation method should I use?
faq.answer: Common methods include straight-line and declining balance, and the right choice depends on the asset and your accountant's guidance.

faq.question: Can I automate the monthly depreciation entry?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set up a recurring journal entry so the same depreciation amount posts automatically each period.

faq.question: Does depreciation affect my cash flow?
faq.answer: No, depreciation is a non-cash expense, so it affects your reported profit but not actual cash movement.
